excerpt: Let love and affection for your subjects become the ornament of your heart, that you may not appear to them as a ravening beast, knowing only to tear them asunder.
When the ruler conceals the faults of his subjects, his own guardian suffers punishment less severe than it otherwise would be.
The parable of the world resembles a serpent: smooth and pleasant to the touch, yet filled with a venom that brings death.
All who inhabit the world are absorbed in hope for the coming day, yet none can guarantee it shall come.
